div1.entryFree: Rely, with on, == to lean, to rest on, to depend on: as one --ing on your lordship's will, and not depending on his friendly wish, Gent. I, 3, 61. if thou hast (wit or impudence), r. upon it till my tale be heard, and hold no longer out, Meas. V, 370. for I, thy resolved patient, on thee still r. All's II, 1, 207. bade me r. on him as on my father, R3 II, 2, 25. he doth r. on none (excuse) Troil. II, 3, 173. that destruction which I'll guard them from, if thereon (my good purposes) you r. Ant. V, 2, 133.
